feedback.text: I flatly agree with all the points made in this act, and consider most of them to be of critical importance for the integrity of a fair marketplace, and the mental well-being of the people who interact with the internet, directly or indirectly. There are plenty of people voicing plenty of anecdotes so I'll pitch in with one of mine, too. The hate and fear that is being perpetuated on online platforms is doubtlessly primarily to blame on the algorithms shaped to deliver incendiary content above all others, but even if businesses were to change the tone of the preferred content, the problems of social media would persist due to the addictive and habit forming design of social media platforms. The various ways in which users are compelled and encouraged to interact with these websites daily, and for extended periods of time, should at minimum be looked into, and rules and regulations should be put into place to remove and mitigate the most egregious practices. Thank you for your time, and thank you to the organizers of the Digital Fairness Act!
